data.title: Goldman Sachs Sees Market Warning Signs – But Likes These 2 Stocks
data.description:
Is everything old new again? We had a financial crisis in 2008, and while market moods are better than that these days, some analysts are starting to see some disturbing parallels.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That's the view of Goldman Sachs strategist Peter Oppenheimer, who notes that risk premia in the equity markets are down and are running at levels we haven't seen since the run-up to the 2008 crisis. While that might present a presentiment of the bad old days, Oppenheimer isn't all doom-and-gloom; in a recent note, he warns that what we're looking at is more in line with a correction than a full-out bear market.

While risk may be increasing, the Goldman Sachs stock analysts still see shares worth buying. We've opened up the TipRanks data platform to look at two stocks which the firm likes, even in today's environment. Let's give them a closer look and find out why Goldman Sachs is recommending Buys.

American International Group (AIG)

We'll start in the insurance and underwriting space, with AIG. This company has been around for over a century, and today its global platform is available in more than 200 countries. AIG wrote $23.9 billion in net premiums in 2024, and another $23.8 billion in 2025. The company operates through three main segments: North American Commercial, International Commercial, and Global Personal.

Among the insurance products available through AIG are property and casualty policies, accident and health policies, and financial, life, and liability coverage. AIG also has financial services such as retirement solutions and asset protection. It's all related to risk management and future security.

AIG pays out a regular dividend to its shareholders, and its last declaration, made on February 10, set a March 30 payout of a 45-cent common share payment. At that rate, the dividend annualizes to $1.80 per common share and gives a 2.25% forward yield. Last year, AIG returned $6.8 billion worth of capital to its shareholders; this return was made through $5.8 billion in share repurchases, and $1 billion in dividend payments.

In its 4Q25 report, released in February, AIG reported $670 million in general insurance underwriting income, a total that was up 48% from the prior-year period. The bottom line reported a non-GAAP adjusted after-tax income per diluted share of $1.96, for a 51% year-over-year gain and beating the forecast by 6 cents per share.

Goldman analyst Robert Cox says of this insurance giant, "We see AIG as a company with peer-high earnings growth and an improving ROE over the coming years (similar to CB), while peers will generally face decelerating ROEs and slower earnings' growth due to cyclical pressures. While we do not think AIG is immune by any means to cyclical pressures, AIG's earnings growth is supported by a strong underwriting position, recent accretive deals that should increase operating and premium leverage, and capital flexibility given ongoing sell-downs of its CRBG stake."

He goes on to add about the company's prospects, "AIG's 1.1x P/B (10.4x P/E) valuation is attractive relative to an ROE regression, especially considering our estimates for the trajectory of stronger fundamentals relative to peers. Furthermore, AIG appears amongst the best positioned as it relates to artificial intelligence and other headline risk (including affordability) as an underwriter focused primarily on large account, multi-national, commercial insurance underwriting, and appears to be an AI investment first-mover based on company commentary and disclosure."

All told, these comments support Cox's Buy rating, while his $90 price target implies a one-year upside potential of 12%. (To watch Cox's track record, click here.)

Turning to the general Street view, this stock gets a Moderate Buy consensus rating, based on 17 recent reviews, including 5 Buys and 12 Holds. The shares are trading for $80.07 and the $87 average target price indicates a 9% gain in the year ahead. (See AIG stock forecast.)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: BofA Names Its Top Auto Picks for 2026
data.description:
Bank of America (BAC) has reinstated coverage of the North American auto and auto-tech sector and named General Motors (GM), Ford (F), and Tesla (TSLA) as its top automaker picks for 2026. Analyst Alexander Perry said the sector could outperform expectations this year as automakers adjust to a new regulatory environment and focus more on higher-margin gasoline vehicles.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

BofA Sees Upside for Ford and GM as EV Push Slows

Perry said traditional automakers like Ford and GM could benefit as the industry steps back from aggressive EV targets. In recent years, heavy spending on electric vehicles and strict emissions rules had weighed on profits.

Now, many automakers are putting more focus on gasoline vehicles, especially pickup trucks and larger models that usually carry higher margins. Perry said this shift could lead to higher earnings estimates for both Ford and GM.

He also noted that several companies are delaying or canceling lower-margin EV programs while extending plans for internal combustion engine vehicles. As a result, EV sales could fall more than 20% in 2026 as consumer incentives fade and automakers slow their EV rollout.

Tesla Stands Out in Autonomous Driving

While Ford and GM are BofA's top traditional auto picks, the firm also highlighted Tesla as its top pick. Perry pointed to Tesla's strong lead in self-driving technology and its ability to expand robotaxi services.

He said Tesla's point-to-point driving software remains the most advanced system for consumer vehicles, even as competition in robotaxi services continues to grow.

Why BofA Is Bullish on the Auto Sector

Perry also sees several trends that could support the broader auto sector in 2026. He expects U.S. vehicle sales and North American production to rise, helped by pent-up demand after years of supply limits.

At the same time, the average age of vehicles in the U.S. is about 12.8 years, while miles driven are at record levels. Together, these trends could trigger a new vehicle replacement cycle.

Looking ahead, Perry said the industry is entering a new stage in autonomous driving. The key question is no longer whether the technology works, but how fast it can scale, with self-driving systems and robotaxi services likely to shape the next phase of the auto market.

Which Is the Best Auto Stock, According to Analysts?

Using TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool, we compared Tesla, GM, and Ford to see how they stack up based on Wall Street analyst price targets. The data shows that GM offers the highest upside potential. At the same time, Ford has more moderate upside, while Tesla's average price target suggests a slight downside from current levels.

Investors can dig deeper to decide which auto stock best fits their strategy. Below is a screenshot for reference.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Apple Stock (AAPL) Wins a New 'Street-High' Price Target from Wedbush
data.description:
Apple (AAPL), the tech giant behind the iPhone and Mac, received a new Street-high price target of $350 from top Wedbush analyst Daniel Ives, implying about 34% upside from current levels. He reiterated his Outperform rating on the stock. The five-star analyst believes Apple's latest Mac lineup powered by new AI chips could help drive stronger demand and bring more users into the company's ecosystem.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

It is worth noting that Ives ranks 728 out of more than 12,000 analysts tracked by TipRanks. He has a success rate of 52%, with an average return per rating of 11.90% over a one-year timeframe.

Why Wedbush Sees More Upside Now

Apple recently introduced several new laptops, including updated MacBook Air and MacBook Pro models powered by its new M5 chips, which are designed to improve performance for AI tasks while offering faster memory and longer battery life.

The company also unveiled a new MacBook Neo, a lower-priced 13-inch laptop starting at $599 aimed at more cost-focused buyers. According to Wedbush, the updated lineup is designed to support on-device AI workloads while also expanding Apple's reach across different price points.

Importantly, Ives noted that nearly half of Mac buyers are still new to the platform. That trend suggests Apple is continuing to attract first-time users rather than relying only on upgrades from existing customers.

Wedbush believes the broader lineup, from high-end AI laptops to a lower-priced entry model, could help expand Apple's user base and provide "incremental upside" to Mac sales going forward.

Is Apple a Buy or Sell Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on AAPL stock based on 15 Buys, nine Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average AAPL price target of $306.12 per share implies 17.6% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Gap Stock Sinks on Cautious Sales Outlook as Tariffs Hit 2025 Profit
data.description:
Gap's (GAP) shares plunged more than 7% early Friday after the apparel and personal care retailer issued a conservative sales forecast for fiscal 2026. The company reported fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 results on Thursday, with quarterly and full-year profit down as tariff headwinds weighed on margins.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During the three-month period that ended on January 31, 2026, Gap saw its earnings per share drop by about 17% to $0.45, slightly below analysts' consensus of $0.46. This is even though its quarterly and full-year net sales rose by 2% to $4.2 billion and $15.4 billion, respectively. The former matched analysts' estimates.

Specifically, comparable sales — sales transactions recorded at stores opened for at least a year — expanded by 3% year-over-year during the final quarter, with online sales accounting for 42% of the company's total net sales during the quarter.

However, Gap posted a quarterly net income of $171 million, down 17% from $206 million in the year-ago quarter. For the full year, profits dropped 3% to $816 million.

Gap blamed its lower gross margin for both periods on the impact of tariffs. However, CEO Richard Dickson told CNBC in an interview that the tariff situation is "fluid, but not letting us get distracted."
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/6/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Fall on Escalating Iran Conflict, Surging Oil Prices
data.description:
U.S. stock futures traded lower early Friday amid U.S.-Iran tensions and rising oil prices after an earlier drop that boosted sentiment in international stock markets. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.39%, 0.29%, and 0.21%, respectively, at 5:35 a.m. EST on March 6.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the time of writing, benchmark Brent crude futures were up 1.9% to $86.93 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate crude futures were 3.6% higher at $83.93 a barrel. The U.S.-Iran war has entered its seventh day and continues to disrupt global fuel supplies. 

During Thursday's regular trading, the three major averages ended in the red due to geopolitical concerns. The S&P 500, the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite, and the Dow Jones fell 0.6%, 1.6%, and 0.3%, respectively. Overall, the Dow Jones is heading towards its second consecutive week of losses.

Coming to economic releases, investors await the release of February's nonfarm payrolls report today. The market consensus indicates a notable slowdown to about 50,000 job additions, compared with 130,000 in January 2026.

Meanwhile, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.167%. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price traded around $5,087 per ounce on Thursday.

Turning to trending stocks, Marvell Technology (MRVL) shares rallied about 12% in pre-market trading after the semiconductor company reported solid results and outlook, driven by AI-led demand. In contrast, Gap (GPS) stock was down more than 7% after the apparel company missed Q4 earnings expectations.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened higher today as investors digested updates on the Middle East crisis and its impact on oil prices.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Higher

Asia-Pacific markets rose on March 6, despite ongoing tensions in the Middle East. Investors continue to assess the impact of the U.S.-Iran conflict on energy prices.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index rose 1.72%. In China, the Shanghai Composite gained 0.38%, while the Shenzhen Component increased 0.59%. 

Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei gained 0.62%, with the Topix up 0.39%.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Li Auto Stock (LI) Climbs as it Joins EV Rivals in Humanoid Robotics Club
data.description:
Shares in Chinese automaker Li Auto (LI) were in full gear today on reports that it is joining its EV rivals in the drive to develop humanoid robots.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

New Robotics Team

According to reports in Chinese media, Li Auto has been developing robotics products for nearly a year now under an internal team code-named Nexus. The unit is said to be led by He Junpei, a former hardware partner at robotics startup Jiuguang.

It is understood to be preparing to unveil a two-wheeled robot designed for work in manufacturing factories later this year. It is also said to be working on a bipedal robot under the same initiative.

Bipedals, also known as humanoids, aim to replicate human movement – walking, running, and lifting. They are expected to be used in factory settings, but also across other sectors from home care to hospitality and even surgery.

A number of other EV makers are already working on humanoid robots. These include fellow Chinese group XPeng (XPEV), which announced last year it was developing the next-generation IRON humanoid robot, featuring a bionic "bone–muscle–skin" structure with a flexible spine, synthetic muscles, soft full-body skin, and 22 degrees of freedom in its dexterous hands.

The company plans to deploy the IRON robot in commercial settings first and aims for mass production by late 2026.

EV Makers Making Moves

Another EV maker known for its humanoid production is Elon Musk's Tesla (TSLA), which is developing the Optimus humanoid. It plans to unveil the Gen 3 version of Optimus in Q1, which will include major upgrades from version 2.5, including its latest hand design. The Gen 3 is its first design meant for mass production.

EV makers are in the driving seat behind the development of humanoids for a number of reasons. The first is that they are looking to diversify their business models as EV demand stutters and competition increases.

The second is that much of the science and engineering know-how, as well as AI chips, sensors and autonomous navigation, involved in EVs are needed to power a humanoid.

China is the global leader in humanoid robotics. According to recent data from Omdia, Chinese humanoid robot makers dominated the market in 2025, with five firms in the top 10.

The global humanoid robotics market was estimated at $425 million in 2025 and is projected to reach $4.75 billion by 2032.

What are the Best Robotics Stocks to Buy Now?

We have rounded up the best robotics stocks to buy now using our TipRanks comparison tool. As can be seen below, Serve Robotics (SERV) has the biggest upside, according to analysts.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: ServiceNow Stock (NOW) Gains on CEO Comments, New Product Launches
data.description:
ServiceNow (NOW) stock rose about 6% on Thursday after the enterprise workflow automation company announced new artificial intelligence (AI)-powered products for the public sector. Moreover, investors cheered CEO William McDermott's comments that indicated that AI is not a threat but a positive for software companies. Shares of software companies like ServiceNow, Salesforce (CRM), and Microsoft (MSFT) have been under pressure recently due to concerns about potential AI disruption. NOW stock was up more than 1% in Friday's pre-market trading, along with other software stocks, amid easing AI fears.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

ServiceNow Investors Cheer CEO Comments, New Launches

At a Morgan Stanley investor conference held on Thursday, McDermott discussed how AI is enhancing the value of software companies.

"If you have a great system of record, AI is actually making your intrinsic value higher because the data that's in those systems is very important," said McDermott.

Meanwhile, at its annual Government Forum yesterday, ServiceNow launched new AI-powered solutions to deliver mission-critical outcomes for the public sector. Specifically, the company introduced its ServiceNow EmployeeWorks offering, which integrates Moveworks' (acquired by CRM in 2025) conversational AI and enterprise-grade search with ServiceNow Employee Center to create an AI front door for an agency workforce and its mission.

The company also launched Autonomous Workforce, AI specialists that can perform tasks with the "scope, authority, and governance" required in Government Community Cloud (GCC) and National Security Cloud (NSC) environments.

Is NOW Stock a Good Buy?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on ServiceNow stock based on 30 Buys, two Holds, and one Sell recommendation. The average NOW stock price target of $191.20 indicates 59% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju
